title: DONE Create Org File Parser Crate with Orgize Integration

ast.raw-value: DONE Design Org File Parser Crate Architecture
title: DONE Design Org File Parser Crate Architecture

ast: Define the API and structure for a dedicated org file parsing crate that integrates orgize with the addisonbeck.com two-layer architecture.
, design a new crate architecture for org file parsing that bridges the gap between database metadata and org file content in the
. This crate should complement the existing org-roam-db-reader crate by handling the content layer.
: Public interfaces for file reading, parsing, and HTML generation
: Integration with existing error types and Result patterns
: How to test org file parsing with real content from
: How parsed content connects with database metadata queries
Use the "On Working With Orgize" mode research to inform architecture decisions. Focus on practical integration rather than comprehensive feature coverage.

ast.raw-value: DONE Update addisonbeck.com Agent Mode with Crate Architecture Guidance
title: DONE Update addisonbeck.com Agent Mode with Crate Architecture Guidance

ast: agent mode to include crate architecture principles and structure guidance.
agent mode to reflect the architectural decisions made during org-file-parser development. The agent should guide future development with clear crate architecture principles.
: Document the value that crates should have focused, single responsibilities (e.g., org-file-parser for pure parsing, not mixed I/O concerns)

: Document the workspace structure with org-roam-db-reader (database layer), org-file-parser (org string parsing layer), and addisonbeck-com (web orchestration). Include reference to not yet developed crates like "org-file-provider" (for reading org files from the content folder and converting them to strings), and an "org-roam-client" glue crate that takes a node ID and uses all our other crates to 1. Fetch its metadata from the database, 2. Fetch the file from the content folder as a string 3. Parses the string into an object, 4. Returns a combined object with node id, the raw database metadata, and the Rust object parsed from the string content.
: Guidelines for when to create new crates vs extending existing ones, with examples from our parsing vs file I/O decision
: How crates should interact (string interfaces, clear error boundaries, minimal coupling)
: Capture the reasoning behind the pure parsing approach as a reference for future similar decisions
This ensures future development maintains clean architecture and doesn't repeat the mixed-concerns mistakes we identified and corrected.
Create the foundational module that reads org files and generates HTML using orgize crate functionality.

, implement the core orgize integration module that handles file reading and HTML generation. This module should provide clean abstractions over orgize's parsing capabilities while being optimized for the addisonbeck.com use case.
: Transform org content through orgize into structured data
: Convert parsed org content to web-ready HTML
: Handle internal org-roam links and cross-references
: Graceful handling of malformed org files or missing content

ast.raw-value: DONE Create Basic Crate Structure and Dependencies
title: DONE Create Basic Crate Structure and Dependencies

ast: Establish the foundational crate structure with minimal dependencies for pure orgize parsing.
, create the basic crate structure for org-file-parser following the updated

. This crate should be a pure orgize wrapper focused on string → structured data transformation.
Implementation requirements:
: Only orgize, serde, and thiserror from workspace
: Create lib.rs with module structure and crate documentation
: Ensure crate is included in workspace members
Follow the dependency patterns from the updated implementation plan focusing on pure parsing functionality.
Create the fundamental data structures that represent parsed org content without file system concerns.
, implement the core data types for org-file-parser as defined in

. These types should represent parsed org content in a clean, serializable format.
Core data structures:
: Level, title, and optional content fields
: Target ID, link text, and link type
: Id, File, Http, Unknown variants for link categorization
: Debug, Clone, and serde Serialize/Deserialize for all types
Focus on clean, simple data structures without file paths or I/O concerns. Include basic constructor methods and utility functions.
Create focused error types for parsing operations without file I/O concerns.
, implement the error handling types for org-file-parser as defined in

. Focus on parsing-specific errors without file system or network concerns.
Error implementation:
: Consistent error handling with thiserror derive macros
: Standard Result alias for crate operations
: Helper methods for creating common error types
: Unit tests covering error creation and display messages
Keep error types minimal and focused only on string parsing concerns, not file I/O.
Create the core parsing function that transforms org content strings into ParsedOrgContent using orgize.
, implement the fundamental orgize integration for parsing org content strings using patterns from
Orgize Parsing Implementation Patterns Test Document

. This should be a pure function taking string input and returning structured data.
Core parsing implementation:
: Simple struct with static parsing methods
: Core method taking &str and returning ParsedOrgContent
: Use Org::parse() to create orgize AST from content string
: Extract title from #+TITLE keyword or first heading
: Graceful error handling for malformed org content
: Test parsing with various org content examples
Focus on string-to-struct transformation without file I/O. Use orgize examples for robust parsing patterns.
Add HTML generation capabilities using orgize's built-in HTML export functionality.
, implement HTML generation for parsed org content using orgize's HTML export features. Follow patterns from
Orgize Parsing Implementation Patterns Test Document

for clean HTML output.
HTML generation scope:
: Generate HTML from ParsedOrgContent with sensible defaults
: Optional configuration for HTML generation (minimal for now)
: Ensure generated HTML is suitable for web display
: Verify HTML generation with various org syntax elements
Keep HTML generation simple and focused on orgize's capabilities. No custom rendering logic needed initially.

ast.raw-value: DONE Implement Link Extraction and Categorization
title: DONE Implement Link Extraction and Categorization

ast: Extract and categorize different types of org-mode links with focus on org-roam ID links.
, implement comprehensive link extraction using orgize AST traversal patterns from
Orgize Parsing Implementation Patterns Test Document

. Focus on identifying org-roam ID links and other internal references.
Link extraction implementation:
: Parse all links from orgize AST and categorize by type
: Distinguish between id:, file:, http:, and unknown link types
: Basic format validation for extracted links
: Comprehensive tests with various link formats and edge cases
Use orgize's link traversal methods to extract structured link data from parsed content.
Extract org-mode headings with their hierarchical structure and content.
, implement heading extraction using orgize AST patterns from
Orgize Parsing Implementation Patterns Test Document

. Extract heading hierarchy and associated content for knowledge graph navigation.
Heading extraction scope:
: Parse all headings from orgize AST with level information
: Extract level, title, and optional content for each heading
: Associate content blocks with their parent headings when possible
: Test extraction with various heading levels and nested content
Use orgize's headline traversal to build structured heading data from parsed content.
Create easy-to-use convenience functions for common parsing operations.

, implement convenience functions that make the org-file-parser easy to use from calling code. These should provide simple interfaces for the most common operations.
Convenience function scope:
: One-step conversion from org string to HTML string
: Lightweight link extraction without full parsing
: Lightweight heading extraction without full parsing
: Test all convenience functions with typical use cases
Design these as simple, stateless functions that calling code can use without understanding the full API.

title: DONE Add Comprehensive Tests and Documentation

Orgize Parsing Implementation Patterns Test Document
: End-to-end tests with real org content examples
: Use proptest for edge case validation

Ensure the crate is fully documented and tested, ready for addisonbeck-com integration.
Verify that org-file-parser is ready to be integrated and called from addisonbeck-com.

, validate that org-file-parser is complete and ready for integration with addisonbeck-com. Test the integration interface and ensure all functionality works as expected.
Integration validation:
: Verify all planned functions are implemented and tested
: Test calling the parser from mock addisonbeck-com code
: Ensure parsing performance is acceptable for web use
: Verify error types integrate cleanly with calling code
: Ensure documentation is clear for integration developers
: Verify the crate compiles cleanly and all tests pass
This TODO ensures the crate is production-ready before integration with the web application.

ast.raw-value: DONE Integrate Parser Crate with Web Application
title: DONE Integrate Parser Crate with Web Application

ast: Connect the org file parser crate to the Axum web application to serve parsed content on web endpoints.

, integrate the new org file parser crate with the addisonbeck-com web application. This integration should combine database metadata queries with org file content parsing to serve complete web pages. It can just display some test data we hardcode in to a call to the parser.

ast.raw-value: DONE Create Comprehensive Content Processing Tests
title: DONE Create Comprehensive Content Processing Tests

ast: Establish a robust testing framework that validates org file parsing with real addisonbeck.com content.
, create comprehensive tests for the org file parsing crate using real org files from the
directory. This test suite should validate the complete data flow from org files to web-ready HTML.
: Verify org syntax is correctly interpreted (headers, links, code blocks, lists)
: Ensure generated HTML is valid and web-appropriate
: Test internal org-roam link conversion to web links
: Handle malformed files, missing content, and edge cases
: End-to-end validation with database metadata integration
Follow TDD patterns established in the existing crates and ensure tests run reliably in the Nix development environment.

ast.raw-value: DONE Plan Content Processing Performance Optimization
title: DONE Plan Content Processing Performance Optimization

ast: Analyze and plan performance optimizations for org file processing at scale.

, assess the performance characteristics of the org file parsing implementation and plan optimizations for handling multiple org files efficiently. Consider both development and production deployment scenarios.
: How orgize parsing scales with file size and count
: Which parsed content should be cached and for how long
: Whether multiple org files can be processed in parallel
: How to handle content changes without full reprocessing
: Performance implications of serving parsed content
Create actionable recommendations for future optimization work based on prototype performance measurements.
